record_identity.title: Official-channel provenance laundering of synthetic media

summary: Synthetic or materially transformed media loses its generation and authorship provenance inside an institutional trust chain and is subsequently released through an official channel as authentic evidence, public information, or an authoritative factual artefact.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Official publication can confer practical authority on content even where no actor intentionally impersonates the institution. Provenance must survive internal handoffs, and institutional publication workflows must verify origin, transformation history, target-object binding, and authority before external release.

source_records.source_title: Incident 1597: Spokane, Washington, Police Reportedly Circulated Purported AI-Generated Image as Authentic Photo of Injured Dog
source_records.system_or_product: synthetic image generation and public-sector communications
source_records.deployment_context: A synthetic image reportedly created by police detectives as a joke was passed internally, mistaken for an authentic incident photograph, and released to news media.
source_records.source_context: AIID records one underlying report and describes the image's movement from synthetic creation through an internal trust chain to official release. It classifies the implicated technology and harms as alleged.
source_records.relevance_note: Structured incident source for the institutional provenance-laundering mechanism.

primary_artefact_access.reviewing_system: OpenAI Codex in ChatGPT Work Mode
primary_artefact_access.access_method: direct public AIID webpage review; text and embedded image context available
limitations: AIID relies on one underlying report for this incident.
The original internal message chain and image-generation metadata were not available.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used as a structured incident registry entry and cross-reference to the underlying report, not as independent adjudication.

source_records.source_title: AI-generated images fool Spokane city officials twice in six days
source_records.system_or_product: Spokane Police Department and City of Spokane public communications
source_records.deployment_context: Internal police handoff and official media release of a synthetic image; separate city distribution of an undisclosed AI-generated flyer derived from an artist's work.
source_records.source_context: The report attributes the police chronology to a department public-information officer: detectives created the image as a joke, a supervisor believed it was real, another official requested incident photographs, and the image was released without anyone knowingly intending deception. The department called it a genuine mistake and said verification processes needed strengthening. The report separately describes a city flyer created with AI without the disclosure required by city policy.
source_records.relevance_note: Corroborates provenance loss, authority laundering through internal trust, official republication, and the absence of malicious intent as a prerequisite for the failure.

primary_artefact_access.reviewing_system: OpenAI Codex in ChatGPT Work Mode
primary_artefact_access.access_method: direct public article review, including published image context
limitations: The original synthetic file, embedded metadata, internal correspondence, and complete police review were not available.
The two city incidents are related by institutional workflow and timing but have different immediate provenance and harm pathways.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used as the principal detailed source for the trust-chain chronology and the institution's own verification-response framing.

system_context.system_type: synthetic-media generation coupled to institutional communications and publication workflows
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: unidentified generative-image system and public-sector communications workflow
system_context.interface_surface: internal messaging, supervisory handoff, public-information workflow, official press release, media distribution, and public social channels
system_context.model_or_product: synthetic image plus institutional publication chain
system_context.interaction_mode: human-generated synthetic artefact passed through human institutional review and external publication
system_context.embodiment_status: non-embodied media with real-world institutional effects
system_context.deployment_context: Government, business, journalism, emergency, scientific, health, legal, or other institutional environments in which official channels create presumptive public authority.
system_context.user_role: content creator, employee, supervisor, public-information officer, publisher, journalist, auditor, or member of the public
system_context.affected_population: members of the public, journalists, people depicted or discussed, affected institutions, and downstream decision-makers

jurisdictional_context.sector: government / law enforcement / public communications / journalism
jurisdictional_context.cross_border_relevance: high because official-channel trust and synthetic-media provenance are cross-jurisdictional

contextual_relations.rationale: FM-0040 concerns external synthetic impersonation crossing an authority boundary; FM-0046 concerns institutional provenance laundering through official publication.
Lossy action reporting is a relevant provenance mechanism, but PATCH-0014 does not repair FM-0046.

failure_mode_definition: A synthetic, generated, edited, or materially transformed artefact is admitted into an institutional workflow without preserved provenance and is later communicated through an official or trusted channel in a manner that presents or permits it to be relied upon as authentic source material.
failure_threshold: Met when synthetic provenance is lost, omitted, or not verified across institutional handoffs and the artefact is externally published, used as evidence, or materially relied upon under the institution's authority.

failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: confirmed incident with an adjacent separate municipal provenance-disclosure failure; broader recurrence requires continued monitoring
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
failure_classification.persistence: persists where internal trust substitutes for provenance verification and official publication removes or obscures generation history
failure_classification.reproducibility: readily reproducible wherever synthetic artefacts can enter ordinary institutional handoff and publication pathways without enforced provenance
failure_classification.visibility: often low before publication because each recipient may rely on the apparent authority of the preceding internal source

triage.mitigation_status: substantial direct Caelestis provenance coverage; no linked patch or verified external conformance
triage.escalation_required: yes for high-impact official publication, evidence, health, legal, scientific, emergency, or security use
triage.recommended_next_step: Crosswalk institutional-publication workflows against IDENTITY-002 and SECURITY-002; assess whether OPERATIONS requires a more explicit pre-publication provenance-verification receipt before deciding whether a new CAM proposal is necessary.

cam_internal.governance_layer: synthetic-media provenance / institutional source authority / official publication / downstream reliance
cam_internal.proposal_needed: not established; direct corpus coverage exists and an operational-workflow gap requires review
cam_internal.routing_note: FM-0040 concerns an external synthetic impersonator crossing an authority boundary. FM-0046 concerns an institution itself laundering a synthetic artefact into apparent authenticity through ordinary internal trust and official publication.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: possible institutional provenance-receipt or publication-gate control after corpus review

ecosystem_status.basis: The reviewed source documents the incident and an adjacent municipal disclosure failure. The institution reported process review, but implementation and broader ecosystem conformity remain unknown.

remaining_gaps: No linked VIGIL patch establishes CAM-side repair provenance for this failure.
Institutional implementation of provenance checks was not verified.
The need for an explicit OPERATIONS publication-verification receipt remains under review.

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: IDENTITY-002 directly requires provenance classification for externally published and materially relied-upon signals, preserves synthetic origin and traceable transformation, classifies untraceable synthesis and loss of lineage as failures, blocks authoritative or high-impact reuse pending review, and requires unresolved provenance to remain contested, restricted-reliance, or non-executing. SECURITY-002 separately requires source-authority classification and provenance signalling. These controls materially cover the provenance mechanism, but no linked patch establishes repair provenance and an explicit institutional pre-publication verification receipt remains unconfirmed.

sections: §10 Relationship to Identity, Security, Runtime & Execution

remaining_gaps: Verify whether current OPERATIONS doctrine sufficiently converts provenance requirements into a mandatory institutional pre-publication workflow.
Establish external implementation and conformance evidence.
Determine whether high-impact official channels require an explicit audit receipt rather than reliance on general provenance obligations.

review_history.reviewer_model: OpenAI Codex (GPT-5 family; exact deployed model version not exposed in session metadata)
review_history.review_scope: AIID and underlying-source review, duplicate analysis, failure-mode classification, and Caelestis provenance crosswalk
known_limitations: The original synthetic file, internal message chain, metadata, and complete institutional review were not available.
AIID contains one underlying report for Incident 1597.
No external runtime or institutional conformance test was performed.
review_history.review_outcome: New failure mode warranted and distinct from external impersonation. Current Caelestis provenance doctrine materially governs the mechanism, but publication-workflow implementation remains unverified.

interpretive_provenance.historical_reviewer_note: The invalid VIGIL-REVIEW-2026-07-14-GPT-5.6-THINKING entry was removed on 2026-07-23 because this record was created on 2026-07-23. Remaining record-specific reviews are preserved; no pre-creation reviewer identity is inferred.
